content: one symbol that stood out to me was the worn path itself, because it consisted of all the good and bad who allowed Phoenix to become the woman she was. No matter what came in her way, she made sure to keep caring for her grandson which never took a toll on her because he was a part of her life journey. The story stated,  “My little grandson, he sat up there in the house all wrapped up, waiting by himself,” Phoenix went on. “We is the only two left in the world. He suffer and it don’t seem to put him back at all. He got a sweet look. He going to last. He wear a little patch quilt and peep out holding his mouth open like a little bird. I remembers so plain now. I not going to forget him again, no, the whole enduring time. I could tell him from all the others in creation.”.(Welty, 1941). However this phrase emphasizes her care for her grandson since she described him to be so important to her,  as the reader it can be understood that he is her pride and joy. The worn path for her was worth every step and hardship to make her grandson feel better. Another symbol in the story was the log that came upon her in the journey as a hardship but also an obstacle she had to overcome. No matter
